# Paragon DOM Selectors

All selectors below were verified by analyzing actual saved HTML snapshots from the production Paragon Connect v26.6 platform (Great Plains Regional MLS).

**Preferred approach in Playwright:** use `getByLabel`, `getByRole`, and `getByText` where possible — they're more resilient than CSS classes. The CSS class names in this doc (`css-16biofz`, etc.) are generated by MUI/Emotion and may change on redeploy, so treat them as a fallback, not the primary selector.

---

## URLs

| Purpose | URL |
|---|---|
| Login | `https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonConnect/gprmls/login` |
| Dashboard (post-login) | `https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonConnect/gprmls/dashboard` |
| Residential Search | `https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonConnect/gprmls/searches/property?classId=1` |

Listing detail URLs contain session-bound tokens (e.g. `ssId=728538`). **There are no permalinks by MLS number.** Always route through the search form.

---

## Login Flow

**Username field label:** "Username"  
**Password field label:** "Password"  
**Submit button text:** "Sign In"

Playwright pattern:
```js
await page.getByLabel('Username').fill(process.env.PARAGON_USERNAME);
await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(process.env.PARAGON_PASSWORD);
await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign In' }).click();
```

**Double-login warning dialog:** Sometimes appears after login with text about being "already logged in." Detect and dismiss before proceeding. Look for a "Continue" or "OK" button in a dialog. If the dashboard URL is reached, login succeeded.

```js
// After clicking Sign In, race between dashboard nav and the double-login dialog
await Promise.race([
  page.waitForURL(/\/dashboard/),
  page.getByRole('button', { name: /continue|ok|yes/i }).click().catch(() => {})
]);
```

---

## Residential Search Form

Navigate directly to `.../searches/property?classId=1` to land on the Residential form.

### Primary Fields

Use label-based selectors (preferred):

| Field | Label text | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| MLS # | `"MLS #"` | Exact single-field lookup when user enters MLS# |
| Address Number | `"Address Number"` | Street number (e.g., "3503") |
| Address Street | `"Address Street"` | Street name without suffix (e.g., "152nd") |
| Address Direction Prefix | `"Address Direction Prefix"` | N, S, E, W |
| Street Type | `"Street Type"` | Street, Avenue, Drive, etc. |
| City | `"City"` | Autocomplete |
| Zip | `"Zip"` | Autocomplete |
| Status | `"Status"` | Multi-select. Default includes Active, New, etc. Leave as-is. |

### Raw CSS fallback IDs (from saved HTML, may change)

```
MLS#:                    #MLS\ \#_autocomplete
Address Street:          #Address\ Street_input
Address Direction Prefix: #Address\ Direction\ Prefix_autocomplete
Street Type:             #Street\ Type_autocomplete
City:                    #City_autocomplete
Zip:                     #Zip_autocomplete
Status:                  #Status_autocomplete
```

Note: Address Number had an auto-generated numeric ID (`#750010005003`) that is **not reliable** across sessions. Use the label selector for this one.

### Submit Button

Floating "Search" button, bottom of the form.

```js
await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Search', exact: true }).click();
```

Raw selector fallback: `button.css-1vgirie` with text "Search".

---

## Search Results Page

Each result is an independent card. The page title is "Residential (My Default) - Search Results - Paragon Connect".

**Pagination/virtualization:** The page header shows total count (e.g., "24 Results") but only ~20 cards are in the DOM at once due to React virtualization. For address searches that might return many hits, the scraper may need to scroll to load more. For our use case (exact address lookups), the target listing is almost always in the first batch.

### Card Container

```
<main class="css-247yzz" role="group" aria-labelledby="listing-title-{MLS_NUMBER}">
```

Playwright pattern:
```js
const cards = page.locator('main[aria-labelledby^="listing-title-"]');
```

### Extractable Fields Per Card

| Field | Selector | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| MLS # | Parse from `aria-labelledby` attribute: `listing-title-22610516` → `22610516` | Also appears in `<p>` aria-label |
| Address | `h6#listing-title-{MLS}` text content | e.g., "7105 Pine Drive" |
| City/State/Zip | First `<p>` with aria-label matching `", NE ##### ."` | e.g., "La Vista, NE 68128" |
| MLS# display | `<p>` with `aria-label="MLS number {MLS}. Residential."` | Duplicate of MLS# for display |
| Square footage | `<p>` with text "Total Finished SqFt - X" | May not appear for all listings |
| Status badge | `<div aria-label="{STATUS}. .">` | Values ALL CAPS: NEW, ACTIVE, PENDING, SOLD, EXPIRED, WITHDRAWN |
| Price | `<div>` containing `$` as first char | e.g., "$325,000" |
| Beds | `<div aria-label="{N} Beds.">` | |
| Baths | `<div aria-label="{N} Baths.">` | |
| DOM (Days on Market) | `<div aria-label="{N} DOM.">` | |
| Cover photo URL | `<img role="link" aria-labelledby="listing-title-{MLS}">` src attribute | Only the first photo — use this for the stored cover_photo_url |

**Status normalization:** Card shows ALL CAPS ("NEW", "ACTIVE"). Detail page shows proper case ("New", "Active"). Normalize to proper case when storing.

### Clicking Into a Listing

The clickable element is the `<img role="link">`. Clicking it navigates to the listing detail page.

```js
// Click the cover image of a specific MLS result
await page.locator(`img[role="link"][aria-labelledby="listing-title-${mls}"]`).click();
```

**Selecting the right result from multiple matches:** When searching by address, prefer Status = Active. If no Active, take the most recent by date. For searches by MLS#, there's typically only one result.

---

## Listing Detail Page — "All Fields Detail"

The listing detail page has a Property Details accordion containing a pink "All Fields Detail" link. **Click it to expand the full field table on the same page** (no navigation — this is JS-rendered in place).

### Trigger

Look for a clickable element with text "All Fields Detail" inside the Property Details area. After clicking, wait for a field-table element to appear before scraping.

```js
await page.getByText('All Fields Detail', { exact: false }).click();
await page.waitForSelector('p.css-bbdbyg:has-text("All Fields Detail")');
```

### Section Structure

The expanded view is a series of sections. Each section has:

- **Section header:** `<p class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body1 css-bbdbyg">SECTION_NAME</p>`
  - Observed section names include: "All Fields Detail", "General", and more depending on the listing.

### Label/Value Pair Pattern

This is the core pattern for scraping all 138+ fields. Labels and values sit inside sibling grid cells and appear **in alternating order**.

**Label:**
```html
<div class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body2 css-16biofz" weight="600" data-left="true">FIELD_LABEL</div>
```

**Value:**
```html
<div class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body2 css-1gmb2pb" data-left="true">FIELD_VALUE</div>
```

Both sit inside `.css-eryr19` grid cells. The pairs are sequential.

### Extraction Strategy

Either traverse the DOM sequentially pairing `css-16biofz` (label) with the next `css-1gmb2pb` (value), or use this regex:

```js
const html = await page.content();
const pattern = /<div class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body2 (css-16biofz|css-1gmb2pb)"[^>]*data-left="true">([^<]*)<\/div>/g;
const pairs = [];
let label = null;
for (const m of html.matchAll(pattern)) {
  if (m[1] === 'css-16biofz') {
    label = m[2].trim();
  } else if (label !== null) {
    pairs.push([label, m[2].trim()]);
    label = null;
  }
}
```

**Verified on sample listing (MLS 22610267):** 138 label/value pairs extracted cleanly including MLS#, Status, Listing Price, Address, County, City, Zip, State, Listing Agent, Listing Office, List Date, Bedrooms, Bathrooms, Garage Spaces, Main Floor SqFt, Basement, Finished Basement, Year Built, Public Remarks, Agent Remarks, Showing Instructions, Lock Box Provider, Longitude/Latitude, Legal Description, Tax ID, and more.

See `samples/all-fields-detail.json` for a complete extracted example.

### Confirmed Field Labels Available

Full list of labels seen on the sample listing: MLS #, Status, Class, Listing Price, Property Subtype, Address, County, City, Zip, State, VOW Include, VOW Address, VOW Comment, VOW AVM, Parcel Number, Listing Agent, Listing Office, Subdivision, List Date, Occupant Type, SID Y/N, HOA (Y/N), Directions to Property, Lot Dimension Source, Lot Dimensions, Total Acres, Bedrooms, Bedrooms Above Grade, Bathrooms, # of Fireplaces, Garage Spaces, Square Footage Source, Main Floor SqFt, 2nd Floor SqFt, 3rd Floor SqFt, 4th Floor SqFt, Basement, Finished Basement, Total Basement SqFt, Total Finished Below Grade, Zoning, Lot Size, Lot, Longitude/Latitude, Legal Description, Year Built, Air Conditioning, Total Square Feet, Bathrooms Full/Half, Stories, Exterior Wall, Heat Type, Tax ID, Lock Box Provider, Showing Contact Type, Agent Remarks, Public Remarks, Showing Instructions.

Other listings may have additional fields (school district, specific taxes, HOA fees, etc.). Scrape everything and store it all.

---

## Documents Accordion

Located below the main listing content on the detail page. Must be clicked to expand before scraping.

### Accordion Heading

```html
<p class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body1 css-19iygoh">Documents</p>
```

The containing `<button>` with `aria-expanded` is the click target. Playwright pattern:

```js
await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Documents' }).click();
await page.waitForSelector('a[href*="AssociatedDocs"]');
```

### Document Row Structure

Each PDF is an `<li>` inside a `<ul>` inside a `<div index=N>` wrapper. One PDF per row.

```html
<ul>
  <li>
    <div>
      <a href="https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonLS/Files/AssociatedDocs/gprmls/9/gprmls_1126824.pdf" target="_blank">
        <svg>...</svg>  <!-- PDF icon -->
      </a>
    </div>
    <div>
      <span>
        <a href="https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonLS/Files/AssociatedDocs/gprmls/9/gprmls_1126824.pdf" target="_blank">LBP</a>
      </span>
      <div>
        <span>216.25 KB</span>
        <div>
          <svg>...</svg>
          <span>Added 02/13/2026</span>
        </div>
        <div>
          <span>Public</span>  <!-- or "Private" -->
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </li>
</ul>
```

### Fields to Extract Per Document

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| URL | First `<a href>` with `AssociatedDocs` in path |
| Name | Text of the second `<a>` (e.g., "LBP", "SPCDS & Soil") |
| Size | `<span>` containing "KB" or "MB" |
| Date Added | `<span>` starting with "Added " |
| Visibility | `<span>` with text "Public" or "Private" |

### PDF URL Format

`https://gprmls.paragonrels.com/ParagonLS/Files/AssociatedDocs/gprmls/{id}/gprmls_{fileId}.pdf`

PDFs are publicly accessible at these URLs without authentication. Store the URL; don't re-download unless/until that feature is added.

---

## Property History Accordion

Below Documents on the detail page. Must be clicked to expand.

### Accordion Heading

```html
<p>Property History</p>
```

Click the containing button to expand. The accordion contains one or more MLS groups, each showing that MLS number's event timeline.

### MLS Group Structure

Each MLS group starts with an `<h6>` header:

```html
<h6 class="..." weight="600">22604189</h6>
```

### Year Marker

Within each MLS group, year transitions are marked by:

```html
<div class="css-rbs0uv">2026</div>
```

Year markers appear in reverse chronological order (most recent first).

### Event Block

Each event has two visual parts that should be scraped together:

**Date/time header:**
```html
<p>Mar<h6>3</h6></p>
<div>12:02 AM</div>
```

**Event detail pane:**
```html
<div class="css-6s8rp0">
  <p>Status: ACTIVE</p>
  <p><span>General Date: </span>2/13/2026</p>
  <p><span>Price: </span>$334,500</p>
</div>
```

### Event Types Observed

- `Status: ACTIVE` / `Status: NEW` / `Status: PENDING` / `Status: SOLD` / `Status: EXPIRED` / `Status: WITHDRAWN`
- `First entry` (original listing creation)
- `Current Entry` (older historical records)
- `Listing Price` (price change)
- `Listing Price,Available to Show Date` (bundled change)

### Extraction Strategy

Walk the DOM forward, maintaining running state for current MLS#, current year, and current month/day/time as you see those markers. For each event detail pane (`.css-6s8rp0`) encountered, emit an event record using the current running state.

See `samples/property-history.json` for a complete extracted example.

### For the AI

Per `docs/01-decisions.md`, only a **summary** goes to Claude: prior MLS#s with their final status and final price. The full timeline is still stored in SQLite for display in the UI.

---

## Summary: What Plays Well with Playwright

- Label-based selectors (`getByLabel`) for form inputs
- Role-based selectors (`getByRole('button', { name: '...' })`) for buttons
- Text-based selectors (`getByText('All Fields Detail')`) for accordion triggers
- `aria-labelledby` attribute parsing for MLS numbers on result cards
- Regex or DOM walk on the rendered HTML for the big label/value pair extraction

## What to Avoid

- Raw MUI/Emotion CSS classes (`css-16biofz`, etc.) as primary selectors — they change on redeploy. Use them only as fallback or within larger structural queries.
- Auto-generated numeric IDs like `#750010005003` — these change per session.
- Assuming listing URLs are stable — session tokens change every login.

## One Gotcha: HTML Entities

If you extract text using regex on raw `page.content()` HTML, you'll get encoded entities like `&amp;`, `&nbsp;`, `&#39;`, etc. If you use Playwright's `locator.textContent()` or `innerText()`, decoding is automatic.

The Paragon listings do contain such entities: e.g., one sample PDF is labeled `SPCDS &amp; Soil` in the raw HTML, which should be stored as `SPCDS & Soil`.

If using regex on raw HTML, run the extracted string through an HTML-decode step (`he.decode(str)` in Node, using the `he` package, or a small custom function).
